response.justification: The candidate is a highly experienced full-stack and web developer, but is fundamentally mismatched for this role on multiple hard requirements. Most critically, the candidate lists no Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — English, Romanian, and basic German only — making them ineligible for a position that mandates native-level Japanese. On the technical side, C programming experience is minimal and confined to a single six-month role at National Instruments with no embedded or kernel context; the candidate's background is overwhelmingly TypeScript, Node.js, and web frameworks. There is no evidence of security domain knowledge (MACsec, 802.1X, captive portal), Layer-2/Layer-3 networking, Linux kernel modification, or embedded device work.
strengths: 15+ years of software development across diverse platforms including some embedded and low-latency systems work
Demonstrated ability to work with Linux-based infrastructure and low-level asynchronous I/O (DLang/Vibe.d, fibers)
Strong engineering discipline with TDD, CI/CD, and craftsmanship practices that transfer to any domain
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency at any level — a stated non-negotiable hard requirement for this position
C programming experience is negligible (one 6-month role); no evidence of embedded firmware, kernel-level, or switch/chip development
Zero exposure to security protocols relevant to the role (MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, MAC authentication, NETCONF, SNMP)
